pages.text_excerpt: How I can Help - Mike Reader MP Skip to content Home Find Help News About Insights Contact Home Find Help News About Insights Contact Stay updated on social media Facebook-f X-twitter Youtube Instagram HOW TO GET HELP Scroll to find out about how I can help Please note that due to strict Parliamentary protocol, I am only able to make enquiries on behalf of my own constituents. To check if you live in my constituency, check your MP here How I can help: I can highlight constituent concerns by asking questions, initiating debates, or lobbying for policy changes. I can help resolve problems with public services like benefits, housing, immigration, or healthcare by contacting relevant government departments or agencies. I can write letters, attend meetings, or apply pressure on local councils or organisations to address specific issues. What I can't do: I cannot intervene in disputes between private parties, such as employer-employee conflicts or custody cases. I cannot influence court rulings or intervene in legal cases already decided by the judiciary. I cannot resolve matters outside of my jurisdiction, such as visa or immigration issues involving foreign governments. GET IN TOUCH Email Me mike.reader.casework@parliament.uk Book an Appointment I run weekly constituency surgeries for face-to-face support. pages.text_excerpt: About Me - Mike Reader MP Skip to content Home Find Help News About Insights Contact Home Find Help News About Insights Contact Stay updated on social media Facebook-f X-twitter Youtube Instagram ABOUT ME I’m proud to be your MP for Northampton South. With 20 years’ experience working in the construction sector globally, Mike brings real-world expertise to Parliament. A local resident in Northampton, he’s fighting for a greener, fairer and more prosperous Northampton. Stay updated on social media Facebook-f X-twitter Instagram Linkedin BIOGRAPHY I grew up in a family of teachers and educators, where public service was a core value. This dedication to helping others shaped my own path to public office. For nearly 20 years, I worked in the construction and infrastructure sectors, leading teams that delivered major projects across the globe. As a civil engineer by training, I’ve seen first-hand how smart investment in infrastructure can transform communities and create opportunities. Speaking up for Northampton in Parliament Northamptonshire has been my home for two decades, and I’ve watched with growing frustration as our town has faced declining public services, Tory scandal after Tory scandal, and missed opportunities. I live in Northampton and believe we desperately need change – to revitalise our town centre, deliver the homes, GP surgeries and NHS dentists we need, and create stable, well-paying jobs for local people. That’s why I chose to stand for Parliament. I want to use my real-world experience of delivering major projects to make a difference for our community. Whether it’s fighting for better NHS services, securing investment in green industries, or ensuring Northampton gets its fair share of funding, I’m committed to being a strong voice for our town. Supporting constituents when they need help In Parliament, I chair the All-Party Parliamentary Groups on International Trade and Investment, Food and Drink, Infrastructure and I am the Business Champion for the Construction sector. I’m a delegate on behalf of the UK to the Council of Europe and was one of the few MPs selected for the Armed Forces Parliamentary Scheme and the NFU’s Food and Farming Fellowship. I’m passionate about making politics work better for ordinary people. My experience in the private sector has taught me the importance of delivering results, not just making promises. As your MP, I will focus on practical solutions and getting things done for Northampton South. Meeting apprentices via the Armed Forces Parliamentary Scheme JOURNEY 2007 Starting my career in construction Mike graduated from Loughborough University with an honours degree in Civil Engineering. 2015 Joining the Labour Party After seeing the failure of the Tory-Lib Dem Coalition, I Joined the Labour Party. 2016 Gaining industry recognition Chosen by industry peers to be the recipient of the UK Constructing Excellence Young Achiever Award 2022 Honing my campaigning credentials Selected for Labour’s Future Candidates Programme which was designed to empower people from outside the ‘political bubble’ to stand for Change. 2023 Selected to stand for the General Election Labour Members from Northampton South selected Mike to represent them. 2024 Elected to serve Mike was elected on 4th July 2024 with a majority of 4,071, overturning a notional majority of 13,000. 2024 Leading change Elected as chair of the Food and Drink, International Trade and Infrastructure APPGs. Find Help 01604 969523 mike.reader.mp@parliament.uk Mike Reader MP, House of Commons, London, SW1A 0AA Latest Latest News Videos In the media Insights All News About Biography Journey Contact Book appointment Meeting & events Social Facebook-f X-twitter Instagram Linkedin Follow me on social media Facebook-f X-twitter Instagram Linkedin Privacy Policy Cookies Policy Terms of Use Sign up to my newsletter Keep up to date with my work as your MP. pages.text_excerpt: Contact - Mike Reader MP Skip to content Home Find Help News About Insights Contact Home Find Help News About Insights Contact Stay updated on social media Facebook-f X-twitter Youtube Instagram Contact Scroll to find out about how I can help Please note that due to strict Parliamentary protocol, I am only able to make enquiries on behalf of my own constituents. To check if you live in my constituency, check your MP here Here’s how I can help you: As your MP I can help with a variety of issues and concerns. First steps Keeping the issue private, I can write to the relevant department, official, or Minister or make a personal appointment to discuss the issue. This can often go a long way to providing a solution to your issue. Making the issue public I can also help to make the issue public by raising it in the House of Commons, where it will be officially recorded, and could potentially come to the attention of the press and public. Outside Parliament Outside Parliament, you can request that I speak at an event concerning your issue, pledge my support to a campaign or write to the local media on your behalf. Book an appointment I hold weekly advice surgeries. They are by appointment only. To make an appointment please send an email using the contact form. Email Me mike.reader.casework@parliament.uk Book an Appointment I run weekly constituency surgeries for face-to-face support. Book a Surgery Appointment CONTACT MY OFFICE My team in Northampton South can often help without you having to attend an advice surgery. Contact my constituency office via one of the ways listed below and a member of the team will get in touch. Contact Details Email address for constituency matters and support mike.reader.casework @parliament.uk Email address for parliamentary queries mike.reader.mp @parliament.uk Address House of Commons, London, SW1A 0AA Leave a voicemail 01604 969523 Find Help 01604 969523 mike.reader.mp@parliament.uk Mike Reader MP, House of Commons, London, SW1A 0AA Latest Latest News Videos In the media Insights All News About Biography Journey Contact Book appointment Meeting & events Social Facebook-f X-twitter Instagram Linkedin Follow me on social media Facebook-f X-twitter Instagram Linkedin Privacy Policy Cookies Policy Terms of Use Sign up to my newsletter Keep up to date with my work as your MP.
